items.title: As Trump Considers Second Iran Attack, It Could Be Deadlier Than the First
items.description: Within days of the U.S. strikes on Iran’s nuclear facilities last year, all sides agreed to a cease-fire. This time could be different.

items.title: In Ukraine, Senators See War’s Impact and Press for Stronger U.S. Support
items.description: Senate Democrats traveled to Kyiv and Odessa to show solidarity with the war-torn nation and make the case that the United States should do more, incl...

items.title: Former Prince Andrew’s Arrest Upends Royal Family’s Effort to Move Past His Scandal
items.description: King Charles III’s family, long rocked by infighting and grievous losses, is facing what could be the gravest threat to its moral authority in more th...

items.title: A Cancer Detection Test Fails in Major Study
items.description: A closely watched clinical trial in Britain that screened blood for early detection of cancer did not show a reduction in later stages of the disease.

items.title: A New U.S. Blockade Is Strangling Cuba
items.description: An analysis of ship movements shows that the Trump administration is isolating the island at one of its most vulnerable moments.
